alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Allergen List - Mandatory declaration of allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. As a general labelling regulation for prepackaged foods, GSO 9:2022 is expected to include requirements for the declaration of allergens. The specific list of allergens and declaration rules must be verified within the full text of the regulation.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Additive Limits - Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies them as unlikely to cause harm to human health. Verification required.. Specific additives and their permitted uses must be verified against MHLW specifications.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and animal pharmaceuticals
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must not exceed quantities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm to human health, unless specific residual limits are established. Verification required.. Specific residual limits for agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and animal pharmaceuticals must be verified against MHLW specifications (Positive List System).

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Toxic or harmful substances, foreign substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if containing or bearing toxic or harmful substances (unless MHLW specifies harmless) or likely to cause harm due to contamination or foreign substances..

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Pathogen contamination
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if contaminated with pathogens and likely to cause harm to human health..

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be assessed and permitted by SFA. Only SFA-assessed and permitted food additives are allowed for use in food products sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Non-permitted Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from import or sale. If a food product contains an additive that is not permitted, it must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (Incidental Constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits. Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants exceeding limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from import or sale. Food products containing contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed regulatory limits must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Diethylstibestrol, Hexoestrol, Dienoestrol residues
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of these compounds.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Declaration of Ingredients and Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Check official source. New: Must be declared on food labels. All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products for sale in Singapore must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Labelling Requirement - General Labelling Compliance
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Check official source. New: Must comply with labelling requirements. Prepacked food products must comply with general labelling requirements.

alerts.title: Modified: Taiwan Allergen List - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Mandatory declaration. New: Mandatory declaration. Mandatory labeling is required if these allergens are present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods, including seasoning and snack products.

alerts.title: Modified: Taiwan Allergen List - Sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: 10 or more. New: 10. Mandatory declaration is required if sulphite as residue sulphur dioxide is present at 10 mg/kg or more in prepackaged foods, including seasoning and snack products.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Food and Nutrition Labeling
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance required. Food products must adhere to general food labeling and specific nutrition labeling requirements.

alerts.title: Modified: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited. New: Not permitted. Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food are not permitted.

alerts.title: Modified: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame. New: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.. These nine foods are identified by law as major food allergens in the U.S. The addition of sesame was effective January 1, 2023.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is an Action Level for total aflatoxins in general foods.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is an Action Level for total aflatoxins in peanuts and peanut products.

alerts.title: Modified: United States Labelling Requirement - "Gluten-free" claim
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Must meet FDA's defined standard. New: Defined standard for gluten content. The FDA has a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling, including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (effective 08/12/2020).

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen preventive controls
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory written procedures. Facilities must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117).

alerts.title: Modified: United States Labelling Requirement - Cochineal extract and carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Must be declared on labels. New: Mandatory declaration on label. Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of major food allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. Foods or ingredients containing a 'major food allergen' must be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

alerts.title: Modified: United States Labelling Requirement - FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Must be identified on labels. New: Mandatory identification on label. All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Method of major food allergen declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. The allergen's food source must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after/next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Specific declaration for tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. The type of tree nut, and the species of fish and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Sulfiting agents
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: ≥10. Various sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food at a concentration of 10 parts per million or more total sulfites.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via GSFA Online Database. New: Verification required via GSFA Online Database (Codex STAN 192-1995). The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels. New: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants in food and feed commodities to protect human health, based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins in Spices
alerts.details: Old: Follow Code of Practice for prevention and reduction. New: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017). This Code of Practice provides guidance for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxin contamination specifically in spices.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, and process hygiene criteria for foods placed on the market.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation sets out the general requirements for the labelling of all prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details regarding mandatory information, format, and language must be verified within the full text of the regulation.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Salt / Sodium Limits - Declaration of salt/sodium content
alerts.details: New: Verification required. GSO 9:2022, as a general labelling regulation, is expected to cover nutritional information, including the declaration of salt or sodium content. Specific requirements for declaration and any potential limits or guidelines must be verified within the full text of the regulation.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Additive Limits - Halal compliance for ingredients and additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for Halal food, which includes criteria for ingredients and processing, indirectly impacting additive choices.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged food products.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - Halal food general requirements
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - Labelling requirements for prepackaged food

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Labelling Requirement - Mandatory expiration dates
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation sets microbiological limits for various food products and ingredients. Specific limits are detailed within the full standard.

alerts.title: Removed: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: India Additive Limits - Permitted Food Additives and their Maximum Permitted Levels (MPL)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required against FSS (Food Products Standards and Food Additives) Regulations, 2011 and subsequent amendments.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: India Allergen List - Mandatory Allergen Declaration
alerts.details: Old: Verification required for specific allergens and declaration format as per FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: India Labelling Requirement - Front-of-Pack Labelling (FoPL) for High Fat, Sugar, Sodium (HFSS)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required for thresholds and warning format as per FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: India Labelling Requirement - General Mandatory Labelling Information
alerts.details: Old: Verification required for all mandatory labelling elements as per FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.details: New: Must conform to Japan's Specifications and Standards for Food Additives. Verification required.. Specific specifications and standards for food additives must be verified.

Specific criteria and standards for food and additives must be verified against MHLW publications, such as 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II Chemical Preservatives (multiple)
alerts.details: New: Calculation tool must be used. When two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used to determine permitted levels.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food Additive Limits
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Different limits apply depending on the additive and the type of food products. Refer to Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.

alerts.details: New: Verification required. Food businesses must use the SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool to check if additives are allowed.
